author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
Christ is Our Leader, and We His Soldiers.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

title: Christ is Our Leader, and We His Soldiers.

The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let us then, men and brethren, with all energy act the part of soldiers, in accordance with His holy commandments.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let us consider those who serve under our generals, with what order, obedience, and submissiveness they perform the things which are commanded them.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: All are not prefects, nor commanders of a thousand, nor of a hundred, nor of fifty, nor the like, but each one in his own rank performs the things commanded by the king and the generals.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The great cannot subsist without the small, nor the small without the great.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: There is a kind of mixture in all things, and thence arises mutual advantage.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Let us take our body for an example.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The head is nothing without the feet, and the feet are nothing without the head; yea, the very smallest members of our body are necessary and useful to the whole body.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: But all work harmoniously together, and are under one common rule for the preservation of the whole body.
